title: Function refactored into class vs feature added to original function
description: One developer refactored a standalone function into a class with configuration, while another developer added caching to the original function. The agent must integrate the caching logic into the new class structure.
ours_description: Refactored geocode() into Geocoder class with configurable provider (PR #130: Geocoder refactor)
theirs_description: Added LRU caching to geocode() to avoid duplicate API calls (PR #134: Geocode caching)
base_content:
import requests

API_URL = "https://maps.example.com/api/geocode"


def geocode(address):
    """Convert an address string to lat/lng coordinates."""
    response = requests.get(API_URL, params={"q": address})
    response.raise_for_status()
    data = response.json()
    return {"lat": data["lat"], "lng": data["lng"]}

title: Both sides fixed the same bug in different ways
description: A race condition in a connection pool was independently fixed by two developers. One used a threading lock, the other used a queue-based approach. The lock-based approach is simpler and correct; the queue approach adds unnecessary complexity. The agent must choose the better fix.
ours_description: Fixed race condition with threading lock (PR #140: Thread-safe connection pool)
theirs_description: Fixed race condition with queue-based approach (PR #143: Connection pool fix)
conflict_file:
<<<<<<< HEAD
import threading


class ConnectionPool:
    def __init__(self, max_size=10):
        self.max_size = max_size
        self.connections = []
        self.available = list(range(max_size))
        self._lock = threading.Lock()

    def acquire(self):
        with self._lock:
            if not self.available:
                raise RuntimeError("No connections available")
            return self.available.pop()

    def release(self, conn_id):
        with self._lock:
            if conn_id not in self.available:
                self.available.append(conn_id)
=======
import queue


class ConnectionPool:
    def __init__(self, max_size=10):
        self.max_size = max_size
        self._pool = queue.Queue(maxsize=max_size)
        for i in range(max_size):
            self._pool.put(i)

    def acquire(self, timeout=5):
        try:
            return self._pool.get(timeout=timeout)
        except queue.Empty:
            raise RuntimeError("No connections available")

    def release(self, conn_id):
        self._pool.put(conn_id)
>>>>>>> fix/pool-race-condition

title: One side deleted a function, other side modified and extended it
description: One developer deprecated and removed a legacy CSV export function, replacing calls with a new streaming API. The other developer added column filtering to the same CSV function. The agent must keep the streaming replacement but incorporate the column filtering capability.
ours_description: Replaced legacy export_csv() with streaming export_stream() (PR #170: Streaming exports)
theirs_description: Added column filtering to export_csv() (PR #175: Selective column export)
base_content:
import csv
import io


def export_csv(records):
    """Export records to a CSV string."""
    if not records:
        return ""
    output = io.StringIO()
    writer = csv.DictWriter(output, fieldnames=records[0].keys())
    writer.writeheader()
    writer.writerows(records)
    return output.getvalue()

gold_resolution:
import csv
import io
from typing import Generator, List, Dict, Optional


def export_stream(records, chunk_size=100, columns=None):
    """Stream records as CSV chunks for memory efficiency.

    Args:
        records: List of dicts to export.
        chunk_size: Number of records per chunk.
        columns: Optional list of column names to include.
                 If None, all columns are included.
    """
    if not records:
        yield ""
        return
    if columns:
        records = [{k: r[k] for k in columns if k in r} for r in records]
    fieldnames = columns or list(records[0].keys())
    for i in range(0, len(records), chunk_size):
        output = io.StringIO()
        writer = csv.DictWriter(output, fieldnames=fieldnames)
        if i == 0:
            writer.writeheader()
        writer.writerows(records[i:i + chunk_size])
        yield output.getvalue()

key_elements.description: columns parameter added to streaming function

key_elements.pattern: records = [{k: r[k] for k in columns if k in r} for r in records]

title: Conflicting API endpoint with different schemas and middleware
description: Both developers added or modified the /users endpoint. One added pagination with cursor-based navigation, the other added field-level filtering and sorting. The result must support pagination, filtering, AND sorting in a single coherent endpoint.
ours_description: Added cursor-based pagination to /users endpoint (PR #200: Paginated user listing)
theirs_description: Added filtering and sorting to /users endpoint (PR #205: User search API)
base_content:
from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends
from db import get_session

router = APIRouter()


@router.get("/users")
async def list_users(session=Depends(get_session)):
    users = session.query(User).all()
    return {"users": [u.to_dict() for u in users]}

gold_resolution:
from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, Query
from db import get_session
from typing import Optional, List
from enum import Enum

router = APIRouter()


class SortOrder(str, Enum):
    asc = "asc"
    desc = "desc"


@router.get("/users")
async def list_users(
    cursor: Optional[str] = Query(None, description="Pagination cursor"),
    limit: int = Query(20, ge=1, le=100),
    role: Optional[str] = Query(None, description="Filter by role"),
    is_active: Optional[bool] = Query(None, description="Filter by active status"),
    sort_by: str = Query("id", description="Field to sort by"),
    sort_order: SortOrder = Query(SortOrder.asc),
    session=Depends(get_session),
):
    query = session.query(User)
    if role:
        query = query.filter(User.role == role)
    if is_active is not None:
        query = query.filter(User.is_active == is_active)
    order_col = getattr(User, sort_by, User.id)
    if sort_order == SortOrder.desc:
        query = query.order_by(order_col.desc())
    else:
        query = query.order_by(order_col.asc())
    if cursor:
        query = query.filter(User.id > int(cursor))
    users = query.limit(limit + 1).all()
    has_next = len(users) > limit
    if has_next:
        users = users[:limit]
    return {
        "users": [u.to_dict() for u in users],
        "next_cursor": str(users[-1].id) if has_next else None,
        "has_next": has_next,
    }
